case_title: BAYSIDE BOATWORKS, INC. VS. BRIAN RANDOLPH et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRIAN RANDOLPH, SAN FRANCISCO INSURANCE CENTER, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE REFERENCES TO ATTORNEYS FEES, COSTS AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REFERENCES TO THE DEFENDANTS' LIABILITY INSURANCE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS REFERENCE IS IMPROPER AND VIOLATED EVIDENCE CODE 1155. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BRIAN RANDOLPH, SAN FRANCISCO INSURANCE CENTER, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO THE SUPPORT THIS CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INDEMNITY) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO PLEAD FURTHER FACTS REGARDING THE IMPLIED INDEMNIFICATION. THE COURT AMENDS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (CONTRIBUTION AND DECLARATORY RELIEF). DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY), THE MATTER IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 =================== AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION AS FINAL. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. A BROKER'S DUTIES TO HIS/HER CLIENT ARE DEFINED BY NEGLIGENCE LAW. IT AMENDS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION TO GIVE PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO DECLARATORY RELIEF. IT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE AS FINAL AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT SHOULD SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REFLECTING THE COURT'S FINAL RULING.

Appearances:
G. Dennis Rodgers, Esq., Cresswell, Echeguren, Rodgers & Noble, 510-444-1735, for defendants and moving party
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq., Cos, Wooton, Griffin, Hanse & Poulos, LLP, 415-438-4600, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT BRIAN RANDOLPH, SAN FRANCISCO INSURANCE CENTER, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.
The Defendant's Motion to Strike the references to attorneys fees, costs and punitive damages is sustained with leave to amend. The Motion to Strike the references to the defendants' liability insurance is sustained without leave to amend. This reference is improper and violated Evidence Code 1155. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
